rationale: X's suggestion to derive default_components from each pipeline's existing _dit_modules declaration mirrors the substance of dsocek's ground-truth concern (avoid ad-hoc naming lists, drive from structured per-model metadata already computed elsewhere), and is corroborated by Y's independent grep turning up the same _dit_modules/_resident_modules attributes — so it reads as grounded, not fabricated. X also flags a concrete, plausible follow-on gap (text-encoder LoRA is a silent no-op via the same failure mode) with specific file/line anchors. Y's review is a careful but purely confirmatory validation checklist that verifies the diff is safe and converges on 'approve,' matching the trivial outcome of all ground-truth reviews but surfacing zero of the actionable design concerns, leaving it with low recall and low actionability despite solid precision.
